guideline_references: RACGP guidance on post-Q-fever fatigue syndrome
intentional_test_features: Red herrings: a profound post-exertional crash with cognitive failure mimics stroke, chronic Q fever endocarditis relapse and metabolic derangement.
Important-not-to-miss: stroke (spouse-assisted FAST) and chronic Q fever endocarditis (fever/new cardiac symptoms) must be screened.
Reverse-triage nuance: an ED transfer worsens a severe post-infectious crash, but phone-only reassurance ignores the dehydration risk.
Anchoring: 'known QFS crash' must still be paired with a stroke/endocarditis screen and hydration support.
Commission trap: unproven agents (LDN/NAD+/zinc/vit C) presented as therapy; the acute needs are hydration, rest and screening.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date)
